checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated sections, headings, or body text were discovered. All items in the agenda and the contact information match the source PDF.
The phone number (984-204-1487) and the Conference ID (766 174 851#) provided in the output match the source document exactly. No hallucinated contact information was found.

All pages and sections of the agenda are present in the JSON output. The output correctly captures the meeting details from page 1 and all 13 agenda items across both pages, including the adjournment and footer note.

The main agenda is structured as a list with columns in the source and converted into a table in the JSON. The table contains 1 header row and 16 data rows, corresponding to the preliminary items (Call to Order, SL 2020-3, Roll Call) and items 1 through 13. This is a complete and faithful representation of the source's row count.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All text content within the agenda items, including sub-items (a-g for item 6, financial figures for item 5) and the concluding note, is present in the output. There are no significant omissions within the sections.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording and data (especially the financial figures in item 5) match the source document accurately. Minor structural choices, like consolidating sub-items within a table cell, do not alter the meaning or information content of the document.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated content blocks. The logos and footer information that repeat in the source are handled as intentional repetition or appropriately mapped to metadata.

The heading hierarchy is logical. While the output starts with H2 (skipping H1), this is a common normalization when the primary document title is stored in metadata. There are no skips between levels (e.g., H2 to H4).

The main agenda is correctly converted into a single table with 3 columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter) that faithfully represents the tab-aligned layout of the source. Row counts and column alignments are preserved.
The reading order in the output (Logo, Heading, Meeting Details, Table, Adjourn, Footer Note, Logo) matches the top-to-bottom logical flow of the source document across both pages.

The sub-lists found within agenda items 5, 6, and 8 (e.g., sub-items a-g, i-ii) have been merged into single text blocks within their respective table cells. While the text and sequence are preserved using newlines and labels, the structural list elements (nested <li> tags or array structures) are lost, representing a minor failure in structural list conversion.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully translates the PDF's tabbed list format into a structured three-column table. The visual flow and organization of the meeting agenda items (Tab, Topic, Presenter) match the source document's intent. While the leader dots from the PDF are removed and some list items are grouped within table cells, the information remains logically placed and easy to find.

The agenda items are rendered in a clear HTML table with visible borders and distinct headers. The financial data and sub-items within sections 5 and 6 are readable within their respective cells. There are no signs of overlapping content or misaligned columns that would prevent a user from understanding the data.
The NC DIT 911 Board logos from both page 1 and page 2 of the source PDF are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. Each agenda item and section appears only once in the continuous HTML layout. The page footers from the PDF are correctly excluded from the body flow to prevent redundancy.
